patches.find: Note: there IS published research on geomagnetic-atmospheric coupling (e.g., Cnossen 2016, Journal of Geophysical Research: Atmospheres; Lastovicka 2009, Advances in Space Research). These studies show that long-term changes in Earth's main magnetic field can weakly perturb the ionosphere and thermosphere (80–600 km altitude) through electromagnetic forcing. The dome's claim is categorically different: it asserts that the SAA (a surface-level magnetic anomaly) drives the Roaring 40s, which operate in the lower troposphere (0–12 km altitude). Published coupling research operates at altitudes 10–50× higher than the tropospheric westerlies, via mechanisms that decay many orders of magnitude toward the surface. Acknowledging genuine magnetosphere-ionosphere coupling does not support the dome's specific claim — it highlights the altitude gap that makes the claimed mechanism physically implausible.Note: there IS published research on geomagnetic-atmospheric coupling (e.g., Cnossen 2016, Journal of Geophysical Research: Atmospheres; Lastovicka 2009, Advances in Space Research). These studies show that long-term changes in Earth's main magnetic field can weakly perturb the ionosphere and thermosphere (80–600 km altitude) through electromagnetic forcing. The dome's claim is categorically different: it asserts that the SAA (a surface-level magnetic anomaly) drives the Roaring 40s, which operate in the lower troposphere (0–12 km altitude). Published coupling research operates at altitudes 10–50× higher than the tropospheric westerlies, via mechanisms that decay many orders of magnitude toward the surface. Acknowledging genuine magnetosphere-ionosphere coupling does not support the dome's specific claim — it highlights the altitude gap that makes the claimed mechanism physically implausible.Roaring 40s = SAA boundary
patches.rationale: EXP-007: claim field contains TWO duplicated copies of the Cnossen 2016 note prepended to the actual claim. Restore claim to the actual dome assertion.

patches.find: The Roaring 40s (~40°S latitude) is driven by Coriolis force combined with persistent atmospheric pressure gradients resulting from differential solar heating. The SAA's southern boundary varies by definition: the standard IGRF/WMM/CHAOS reference (32,000 nT at sea level) places it at ~45–50°S, which does overlap the Roaring 40s (40–50°S). This latitude coincidence is real — the dome is not fabricating a nonexistent overlap. However, a latitude match is the weakest possible form of evidence for a causal claim, for two independent reasons. In any case, the SAA boundary is a consequence of magnetic field configuration in the core and magnetosphere, while the Roaring 40s are driven by Coriolis deflection from differential solar heating: independent physical causes with no common mechanism. First, independent mechanisms: the Roaring 40s arise from Hadley-cell dynamics and Coriolis deflection; the SAA arises from an eccentric flux concentration in Earth's outer core 2,900 km below the surface. Second, the decisive geographic test: the Roaring 40s encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere at all longitudes — mariners have recorded constant westerlies at 40–50°S across the Pacific, Indian, and Atlantic Oceans since Drake's 1577 circumnavigation. The SAA, by contrast, is confined to approximately 90°W–40°E (roughly one-third of the circumference, centered over South America and the South Atlantic). Strong westerly winds at 40–50°S are observed robustly over the central Pacific and Indian Oceans — thousands of kilometres from the SAA. If a single "aetheric rim pressure at the disc edge" caused both phenomena, the SAA should also extend 360° around the disc edge. It does not. The dome's own code confirms the absence of genuine prediction: monitor.py Domain 27 hardcodes observed='22.5 m/s' with pass=True, and Domain 33 hardcodes observed='48.5°S' with pass=True — static assertions that can never fail regardless of actual measurements. Only Domain 17 (AAO index) accesses live NOAA data, but it tests only whether the current atmospheric oscillation index is elevated, not whether the Roaring 40s are caused by the SAA. Two of three validation domains are unfalsifiable by construction. The SAA, by contrast, is confined to approximately 90°W–40°E (roughly one-third of the circumference, centered over South America and the South Atlantic). patches.rationale: EXP-007: current text has duplicated paragraphs. Replacement is clean with longitude mismatch as primary argument.

patches.find: The dome model uses r = 0 as the mathematical North Pole in its cavity height formula H(r) and as the aetheric source in its toroidal flow model — those functional roles are addressed elsewhere in this review. WIN-031 specifically claims that six ancient texts (Genesis 1:2, Job 37:18, Isaiah 40:22, Genesis 1:6–8, Hildegard of Bingen's Scivias, and related sources) converge on a 'cosmic mountain' at the North Pole, constituting a confirmed 'prediction.' Two problems undermine this: (1) Textual exegesis of pre-existing documents is retrodiction, not prediction — these texts existed thousands of years before the dome model was written. (2) No physical mechanism specifies a measurable height, composition, or observable effect for the mountain that distinguishes dome geometry from any other cosmology whose axis passes through the North Pole.
patches.replace: The dome model claims that six ancient texts (Genesis 1:2, Job 37:18, Isaiah 40:22, Genesis 1:6–8, Hildegard von Bingen's Scivias, and related sources) converge on a 'cosmic mountain' at the North Pole, constituting a confirmed prediction. Two structural problems undermine this. First, textual exegesis of pre-existing documents is retrodiction, not prediction. These texts were composed centuries to millennia before the dome model. Identifying a pattern in ancient literature and declaring it 'confirmed' reverses the arrow of prediction: the model was fitted to the texts, not the texts derived from the model. Second, the 'cosmic mountain at the pole' motif is non-discriminating — it appears in mutually exclusive cosmologies worldwide. Mircea Eliade's systematic survey (The Sacred and the Profane, 1957) documents the axis mundi archetype across Hindu (Mount Meru), Norse (Yggdrasil), Greek (Mount Olympus), Islamic (Mount Qaf), Zoroastrian (Harā Bərəzaitī), and Mesoamerican traditions. These cosmologies are mutually contradictory — they cannot all be correct — yet all feature a sacred center at the world's axis. The dome's six-text convergence proves too much: the same convergence exists for incompatible cosmological frameworks, so it carries zero discriminating power for the dome specifically. The North Pole is genuinely special as Earth's rotational axis and the natural origin for any axially symmetric model. The dome's choice of r = 0 there is geometrically sensible. But the physical functions of r = 0 — the cavity height formula H(r), the toroidal flow source, the coordinate origin — are evaluated under their respective WINs (WIN-002, WIN-009, etc.). WIN-031 adds only the theological label 'cosmic mountain,' which carries no additional testable content. The dome's own monitor.py contains no validation domain for WIN-031, confirming that the author's automated system treats this as untestable.
patches.rationale: EXP-007: WIN-031 too shallow. Expanded with axis mundi cross-cultural evidence, retrodiction framing, and monitor.py absence.

patches.find: Six-text convergence is retrodiction (all texts predate the model by centuries to millennia); no measurable physical consequence distinguishes this from any cosmology with a North Pole axis.
patches.replace: Six-text convergence is retrodiction (all texts predate the model by centuries to millennia). The axis mundi motif appears in mutually exclusive cosmological traditions worldwide, so convergence on a polar sacred center carries zero discriminating power. No measurable physical consequence distinguishes this from any cosmology whose axis passes through the North Pole. The dome's own monitoring system excludes this WIN, implicitly acknowledging it is not a testable prediction.
patches.rationale: EXP-007: Add axis mundi non-discrimination argument and monitoring system exclusion.

patches.find: This is biblical exegesis, not a physical hypothesis. No testable prediction or measurable consequence follows from this claim. The dome’s own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032 in its 39-domain audit engine — if the author considered this a genuine, testable prediction, it would appear in the monitoring pipeline. Its complete absence from the code is a tacit admission that no computational verification is possible for a theological axiom. The dome’s own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032 in its 39-domain audit engine — if the author considered this a genuine, testable prediction, it would appear in the monitoring pipeline. Its complete absence from the code is a tacit admission that no computational verification is possible for a theological axiom. Unlike WIN-031 (a purported physical mountain at the geographic pole), WIN-032 describes a supernatural descent along a geometric axis — doubly unfalsifiable, as it requires both accepting the dome geometry and the prophetic framework. The dome site classifies this as a 'CONFIRMED' prediction alongside empirically measured quantities (Schumann resonances, magnetic field values), which dilutes the meaning of confirmation. The model's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for WIN-032, confirming that 'CONFIRMED' is self-awarded rather than computationally verified. This WIN exemplifies at least 4 theological claims in the 67 that carry zero empirical content yet appear in the denominator of the 95.2% accuracy figure.
patches.replace: WIN-032 claims that the New Jerusalem of biblical eschatology (Revelation 21:2–4) is located at the dome's north pole axis. This commits a category error that our review should name explicitly: counting a future supernatural event as a confirmed prediction. A confirmed prediction in science is a forecast that was tested against measurement and could have failed but did not. WIN-032 describes an eschatological event with no present physical manifestation, no mechanism, no timeline, and no falsification criteria. It is not a prediction awaiting confirmation — it is a theological axiom embedded in the model's architecture. The dome model was designed with the north pole as its central axis; placing the New Jerusalem there is a premise, not a derived consequence. This is the 'Confirming the Premise' pattern: counting a design assumption as empirical support. Moreover, Revelation 21:2 describes the holy city 'coming down out of heaven from God' but specifies no geographic coordinates or axis. The dome author added the pole-axis location — this is post-hoc exegesis, not prediction. The dome's own monitor.py contains no validation domain for WIN-032 among its 39 automated checks, confirming that even the author's engineering treats this as untestable. The dome's broader framework is revealing: context.html links WIN-032 to Temple Mount geopolitical projects and describes the north pole axis as 'the control point of the entire dome system.' This framing confirms that WIN-032 operates within a theological-political narrative, not a scientific one. Counting it alongside empirically measured quantities (Schumann resonances, magnetic field values) in the '67 confirmed predictions' scorecard dilutes the meaning of confirmation. WIN-032 is one of at least four theological assertions (alongside WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-034) that carry zero empirical content yet appear in the denominator of the claimed 95.2% accuracy figure.
patches.rationale: EXP-007: Named category error, Revelation 21:2 exegesis, context.html geopolitical framing, scorecard inflation.

patches.find: Theological assertion, not testable physical claim.
patches.replace: Eschatological prophecy mapped to dome geometry axis. No measurable consequence exists in the present; no falsification criteria are specified; the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author. The dome's own monitoring system excludes WIN-032, and the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics. Counting future supernatural events as confirmed predictions inflates the model's scorecard.
patches.rationale: EXP-007: Expanded from terse 'Theological assertion' to full reasoning.
